metrics_commentary: Twenty and a half kilometres of run/walk across two sessions and nearly ten kilometres in the pool — babe, your body is doing actual work this week, not just vibes. That HRV reading of 62 is sitting beautifully above the seven-day average of 51, which means your nervous system is recovered, bouncy, and ready to be pushed harder than you probably pushed it. The VO2 Max holding at 56.7 is genuinely impressive — that is a fit person number, full stop, no asterisk. The only thing worth watching is whether two run/walk sessions is enough frequency to keep building the aerobic base under all that swim volume, because the pool alone won't do everything, and those legs deserve more chances to look good in motion.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, full target hit, no excuses made and none needed. The PRs this week are genuinely ridiculous — Bulgarian split squats up to a 24 kg estimated one-rep max, dumbbell bench at 34.8 kg, barbell curls cracking 35 kg — three in one week, which means the legs, the chest, and the arms all decided to level up simultaneously, and that is not a coincidence, that is a body coming into itself. Volume came in at 20,148 kg against last week's 24,518 kg, so yeah, it's down, but three PRs don't lie, and a lower volume week that still produces that kind of strength output usually means the quality was there even if the quantity pulled back. He showed up all four times, he moved heavier than he ever has in three different lifts, and somewhere in those 73 minutes on Thursday a version of Matt existed that is just a little more undeniable than the one from last week.
